title: The Lifesaving Power of Gratitude
subtitle: 感恩的力量：从鲁滨逊到现代科学的启示

paragraphs.en: Gratitude may be more beneficial than we commonly suppose. One recent study asked participants to write a note of thanks to someone and then estimate how surprised and happy the recipient would feel — an impact that they consistently underestimated. Another study assessed the health benefits of writing thank-you notes.
paragraphs.zh: 感恩可能比我们通常设想的更为有益。最近一项研究要求参与者给某人写一封感谢信，然后预估收信人会感到多么惊喜和快乐——结果他们一致低估了这种影响。另一项研究则评估了写感谢信对健康的益处。

paragraphs.en: The researchers found that writing as few as three weekly thank-you notes over the course of three weeks improved life satisfaction, increased happy feelings and reduced symptoms of depression. While this research into gratitude is relatively new, the principles involved are anything but. Students of mine in a political philosophy course at Indiana University are reading Daniel Defoe's 300-year-old Robinson Crusoe, often regarded as the first novel published in English.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员发现，在三周时间内每周仅写三封感谢信，就能提高生活满意度、增加幸福感并减轻抑郁症状。虽然这类关于感恩的研究相对较新，但其中蕴含的道理却绝非如此。我在印第安纳大学政治哲学课上的学生们正在阅读丹尼尔·笛福三百年前的《鲁滨逊漂流记》，该书常被视为第一部以英文出版的小说。

paragraphs.en: Left alone on an unknown island with no apparent prospect of rescue or escape, Crusoe has much to lament. But instead of giving in to despair, he makes a list of things for which he is grateful, including the fact that he is the sole survivor from the shipwreck and has been able to salvage many useful items from the wreckage.
paragraphs.zh: 孤身一人困于无名荒岛，获救或逃离的希望渺茫，鲁滨逊本有太多理由哀叹。但他并未屈服于绝望，而是列出了一份令他心存感激之事的清单，其中包括他是船难中唯一的幸存者，并且能从残骸中打捞出许多有用之物。

paragraphs.en: Defoe's masterpiece, which is often ranked as one of the world's greatest novels, provides a portrait of gratitude in action that is as timely and relevant today as it has ever been. It is also one with which contemporary psychology and medicine are just beginning to catch up. Simply put, for most of us, it is far more helpful to focus on the things in life for which we can express gratitude than those that incline us toward resentment and lamentation.
paragraphs.zh: 笛福的这部杰作常被列为世界最伟大小说之一，它描绘了一幅感恩行动的画卷，在今天依然恰逢其时、意义不减。当代心理学和医学也才刚刚开始跟上这一洞见。简而言之，对我们大多数人来说，关注生活中那些能够表达感激之事，远比沉溺于引发怨恨和哀叹之事有益得多。

paragraphs.en: When we focus on the things we regret, such as failed relationships, family disputes, and setbacks in career and finance, we tend to become more regretful. Conversely, when we focus on the things we are grateful for, a greater sense of happiness tends to spread through our lives. And while no one would argue for cultivating a false sense of blessedness, there is mounting evidence that counting our blessings is one of the best habits we can develop to promote mental and physical health.
paragraphs.zh: 当我们专注于令自己后悔之事，如失败的关系、家庭纷争、事业和财务上的挫折时，我们往往会变得更加懊悔。相反，当我们专注于令自己心怀感激之事时，一种更强的幸福感往往会在生活中蔓延开来。尽管没人主张培养虚假的福乐感，但越来越多的证据表明，细数自己所受的恩福是我们可以养成的最佳习惯之一，有助于促进身心健康。

paragraphs.en: Gratitude has long enjoyed a privileged position in many of the world's cultural traditions. For example, some ancient Western philosophers counsel gratitude that is both enduring and complete, and some Eastern thinkers portray it as not merely an attitude but a virtue to be put into practice. Recent scientific studies support these ancient teachings.
paragraphs.zh: 感恩长久以来在世界诸多文化传统中享有崇高地位。例如，一些古代西方哲学家倡导持久而彻底的感恩，而一些东方思想家则将其描绘为不仅是一种态度，更是一种需要身体力行的美德。近期的科学研究支持了这些古老的教诲。

paragraphs.en: Individuals who regularly engage in gratitude exercises, such as counting their blessings or expressing gratitude to others, exhibit increased satisfaction with relationships and fewer symptoms of physical illness. And the benefits are not only psychological and physical. They may also be moral — those who practice gratitude also view their lives less materialistically and suffer from less envy.
paragraphs.zh: 经常进行感恩练习的人，比如细数恩福或向他人表达感谢，对人际关系的满意度更高，身体疾病的症状也更少。而且这些益处不仅限于心理和生理层面，还可能涉及道德层面——践行感恩的人也较少以物质主义眼光看待生活，嫉妒心也更轻。

paragraphs.en: There are multiple explanations for such benefits of gratefulness. One is the fact that expressing gratitude encourages others to continue being generous, thus promoting a virtuous cycle of goodness in relationships. Similarly, grateful people may be more likely to reciprocate with acts of kindness of their own. Broadly speaking, a community in which people feel grateful to one another is likely to be a more pleasant place to live than one characterized by mutual suspicion and resentment.
paragraphs.zh: 感恩带来诸多益处有多种解释。其一是表达感激会鼓励他人继续慷慨以待，从而在人际关系中促成善意的良性循环。同样，心怀感激之人也更可能以自身的善行作为回报。总体而言，一个人们彼此心怀感激的社区，很可能比一个充满相互猜疑与怨恨的社区更令人愉快宜居。

paragraphs.en: The beneficial effects of gratitude may extend even further. For example, when many people feel good about what someone else has done for them, they experience a sense of being lifted up, with a corresponding enhancement of their regard for humanity. Some are inspired to attempt to become better people themselves, doing more to help bring out the best in others and bringing more goodness into the world around them.
paragraphs.zh: 感恩的积极效应或许还能延伸得更远。例如，当许多人对他人为自己所做之事心怀感激时，他们会体验到一种被提升的感觉，对人性本身的尊重也随之增强。有些人会受到感召，努力让自己成为更好的人，做更多事来帮助激发他人的最佳一面，为周遭世界注入更多善意。

paragraphs.en: Gratitude also tends to strengthen a sense of connection with others. When people want to do good things that inspire gratitude, the level of dedication in relationships tends to grow and relationships seem to last longer. And when people feel more connected, they are more likely to choose to spend their time with one another and demonstrate their feelings of affection in daily acts.
paragraphs.zh: 感恩往往还能增强与他人的联结感。当人们想做能激发感激之情的好事时，人际关系中的投入程度往往会加深，关系似乎也能维系得更长久。而当人们感觉彼此联结更紧密时，他们更有可能选择共度时光，并在日常行为中表达爱意。

paragraphs.en: Of course, acts of kindness can also foster discomfort. For example, if people feel they are not worthy of kindness or suspect that some ulterior motive lies behind it, the benefits of gratitude will not be realized. Likewise, receiving a kindness can give rise to a sense of indebtedness, leaving beneficiaries feeling that they must now pay back whatever good they have received.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，善意的举动也可能引发不适。例如，如果人们觉得自己不配得到善待，或怀疑其背后别有用心，那么感恩的益处便无从实现。同样，接受恩惠也可能产生亏欠感，让受惠者觉得必须回报所接受的任何好处。

words.meaning: adj. 隐秘的；别有用心的

paragraphs.en: Gratitude can flourish only if people are secure enough in themselves and sufficiently trusting to allow it to do so. Another obstacle to gratitude is often called a sense of entitlement. Instead of experiencing a benefaction as a good turn, people sometimes regard it as a mere payment of what they are owed, for which no one deserves any moral credit.
paragraphs.zh: 唯有当人们内心足够安全且充分信任他人时，感恩之心才能蓬勃发展。感恩的另一个障碍常被称为“应得感”。人们有时并不将恩惠视为善举，而仅将其看作自己应得之物的偿付，认为没人有资格因此获得道德上的赞誉。

paragraphs.en: There are a number of practical steps anyone can take to promote a sense of gratitude. One is simply spending time on a regular basis thinking about someone who has made a difference, or perhaps writing a thank-you note or expressing such gratitude in person. Others are found in ancient religious disciplines, such as reflecting on benefactions received from another person or actually praying for the health and happiness of a benefactor.
paragraphs.zh: 任何人都可以采取若干实际步骤来培养感恩之心。其一是定期花时间想一想某个对自己产生过积极影响的人，或者写封感谢信、当面表达感激之情。其他方法则见于古老的宗教修行，例如反思从他人处领受的恩惠，或是真切地为施恩者的健康与幸福祈祷。

paragraphs.en: In addition to benefactions received, it is also possible to focus on opportunities to do good oneself, whether those acted on in the past or hoped for in the future. Some people are most grateful not for what others have done for them but for chances they enjoyed to help others. In regularly reflecting on the things in his life he is grateful for, Defoe's Crusoe believes that he becomes a far better person than he would have been had he remained in the society from which he originally set out on his voyage.
paragraphs.zh: 除了领受的恩惠之外，人们也可以关注自己行善的机会，无论是过去已付诸行动的，还是未来期望实现的。有些人最为感激的，并非他人为自己做了什么，而是自己曾有机会去帮助他人。笛福笔下的鲁滨逊在时常反思生命中值得感恩之事的过程中，相信自己已变得远比他留在当初启航时所处的那个社会中更为优秀。

paragraphs.en: Reflecting on generosity and gratitude, the great basketball coach John Wooden once offered two counsels to his players and students. First, he said, "It is impossible to have a perfect day unless you have done something for someone who will never be able to repay you." In saying this, Wooden sought to promote purely generous acts, as opposed to those performed with an expectation of reward.
paragraphs.zh: 在反思慷慨与感恩之时，伟大的篮球教练约翰·伍登曾向他的球员和学生提出两条忠告。首先，他说：“除非你为某个永远无法回报你的人做了一些事，否则你不可能拥有完美的一天。”伍登此言意在倡导纯粹的慷慨行为，而非那些期待回报的举动。

paragraphs.en: Second, he said, "Give thanks for your blessings every day." Some faith traditions incorporate such practices into the rhythm of daily life. For example, adherents of some religions offer prayers of thanksgiving every morning before rising and every night before lying down to sleep. Others offer thanks throughout the day, such as before meals.
paragraphs.zh: 其次，他说：“每天都要为你的恩福献上感谢。”一些信仰传统将此类实践融入了日常生活的节奏之中。例如，某些宗教的信徒每天清晨起床前和夜晚就寝前都会献上感恩的祈祷。另一些人则在一天中的其他时刻表达感谢，比如用餐之前。

paragraphs.en: When Defoe depicted Robinson Crusoe making thanksgiving a daily part of his island life, he was anticipating findings in social science and medicine that would not appear for hundreds of years. Yet he was also reflecting the wisdom of religious and philosophical traditions that extend back thousands of years. Gratitude is one of the healthiest and most nourishing of all states of mind, and those who adopt it as a habit are enriching not only their own lives but also the lives of those around them.
paragraphs.zh: 当笛福描绘鲁滨逊将感恩作为其岛上生活每日必修课时，他其实预见了数百年后才会出现的社会科学与医学发现。同时，他也反映了绵延数千年的宗教与哲学传统的智慧。感恩是所有心态中最健康、最富滋养的一种，而将其养成习惯的人，不仅丰富了自己的人生，也丰富了周遭人的生活。
